description: This pipeline performs image processing and analysis, starting with the detection of spots or objects within an image, followed by filtering and tracking of these objects across multiple frames or dimensions. The pipeline also involves various image transformations, such as merging and splitting of image stacks, application of mathematical operations, and conversion of regions of interest to masks. Additionally, it includes steps for image filtering, blurring, and enhancement, ultimately leading to the generation of output data that represents the analyzed and processed image information.

f5916d86-817d-4804-8e4d-5c81bbe1f5c3.name: ij1-dimensions-zproject-2
f5916d86-817d-4804-8e4d-5c81bbe1f5c3.description: Performs a Z-Projection. Also supports other axes C-Project/T-Project.

467cf542-8f46-4662-8c5d-9ef47d92f2f0.name: ij1-roi-to-mask
467cf542-8f46-4662-8c5d-9ef47d92f2f0.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

16e5cf20-a05d-45ca-b4f1-e16ca6374c08.name: io-interface
16e5cf20-a05d-45ca-b4f1-e16ca6374c08.description: Passes its input to its output without changes.

951c7e2c-debd-46ce-9bd2-4c2b6a12175e.name: ij1-math-imagecalculator2d-expression
951c7e2c-debd-46ce-9bd2-4c2b6a12175e.description:
Applies a pixel-wise mathematical operation that produces a single output image. Please note that this node applies an expression for each pixel, which has a considerable performance cost. We recommend to use 'Fast image arithmetics' instead.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

68ff1280-125c-4773-b128-b56229bbf0d2.name: trackmate-tracking
68ff1280-125c-4773-b128-b56229bbf0d2.description:
Track spots using TrackMate

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

33cd6be1-1f43-445d-92d1-39c9e34a4e20.name: ij1-dimensions-merge-2d-to-hyperstack
33cd6be1-1f43-445d-92d1-39c9e34a4e20.description:
Merges all incoming 2D slices into a hyperstack. The slice positions within the hyperstack can be controlled via expressions. The node can handle missing and negative slice positions due to a remapping procedure. Please ensure that there are no duplicate locations.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of multiple data items. If you want to group all data into one output, set the matching strategy to 'Custom' and leave 'Data set matching annotations' empty.

7d69bd12-2d68-4230-973b-e2529e1e7f85.name: ij1-blur-median2d
7d69bd12-2d68-4230-973b-e2529e1e7f85.description: Applies a median filter. If a multi-channel image is provided, the operation is applied to each channel. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

60bca768-a60f-4e1d-8c31-a9003a331191.name: ij1-dimensions-expression-slicer
60bca768-a60f-4e1d-8c31-a9003a331191.description: Uses expressions to create custom stacks. There are three expressions, one for each Z, channel and frame plane that return numeric indices of slices that should be generated. You can optionally enable to iterate over all incoming slices of the image to generate multiple stacks. If no iteration is enabled (default), each expression is only executed for z=0,c=0,t=0.

71956ffa-86dd-4400-b3a0-d3d9f962de5c.name: trackmate-detector
71956ffa-86dd-4400-b3a0-d3d9f962de5c.description:
Detect spots using TrackMate

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

bcf5cf7a-79ad-4b44-8e24-2392923ad731.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
bcf5cf7a-79ad-4b44-8e24-2392923ad731.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

3d5ace36-ad45-4f9e-9952-62727246cd74.name: ij1-blur-median2d
3d5ace36-ad45-4f9e-9952-62727246cd74.description: Applies a median filter. If a multi-channel image is provided, the operation is applied to each channel. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

f1d43035-baad-4add-9609-c63c6d97e56a.name: cellpose-2
f1d43035-baad-4add-9609-c63c6d97e56a.description:
Deprecated. Use the node with the same name.

Runs Cellpose on the input image. This node supports both segmentation in 3D and executing Cellpose for each 2D image plane. This node can generate a multitude of outputs, although only ROI is activated by default. Go to the 'Outputs' parameter section to enable the other outputs.
  • Labels: A grayscale image where each connected component is assigned a unique value.
  • Flows XY: An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel
  • Flows Z: Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)
  • Flows d: Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)
  • Probabilities: An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel
  • ROI: ROI of the segmented areas.
Please note that you need to setup a valid Python environment with Cellpose installed. You can find the setting in Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose.

This algorithm merges all annotations and data annotations. Use the iteration step settings to determine how annotations and data annotations are merged

7d5fb960-eb86-441a-aa36-0aa4d5f436fe.name: ij1-dimensions-hyper-stacksplitter
7d5fb960-eb86-441a-aa36-0aa4d5f436fe.description: Splits a hyperstack by a selected dimension into a single or multiple output slots. Multiple output slots can be utilized to split specific indices.
